entities.name: PHOSPHATE ADSORPTION
entities.description: The process by which phosphate ions are bound to soil particles.

entities.name: PHOSPHATE DESORPTION
entities.description: The process by which adsorbed phosphate is released back into the soil solution.

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS MANAGEMENT STRATEGIES
entities.description: Approaches to managing phosphorus application and availability in soils.

entities.name: RICE
entities.description: The crop grown in the soils studied in West Africa.

entities.name: WEST AFRICA
entities.description: The geographic region where the soils were sampled.

entities.name: STANDARD PHOSPHORUS REQUIREMENT
entities.description: The amount of P to be added to attain an equilibrium solution concentration of 0.2 mg P l−1.

entities.name: 0.01 M KCL
entities.description: An extractant used to measure the extractability of adsorbed phosphorus.

entities.name: OLSEN P
entities.description: An extractant used to measure the extractability of adsorbed phosphorus.

entities.name: BRAY 1 P
entities.description: An extractant used to measure the extractability of adsorbed phosphorus.

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS AVAILABILITY
entities.description: The extent to which phosphorus is available for plant uptake in the soil.

entities.name: HUMID ZONE
entities.description: The climatic condition of the region in West Africa where the soils are located.
